details: Awarded a competitive $22.5k AI Safety Grant to direct independent research for an upcoming NeurIPS 2026 submission
Investigating the long-term behavior of latent adversarial training under continuous adversarial pressure
Developing jailbreaking techniques and adversarial attacks on LLMs
Orchestrating multi-GPU distributed training on Slurm/HPC for Clipped Importance Sampling Policy Optimization (CISPO)
Selected for a competitive fellowship with a 4% acceptance rate, training alongside PhDs and industry professionals
Implemented Transformers from scratch and completed deep dives into RLHF and interpretability
Built a scalable model-fitting pipeline on Slurm HPC clusters to quantify controllability in behavioral data
Led computational modeling of agency and developed algorithms to extract latent parameters from clinical decision-making data
Awarded grant funding and mentorship to execute independent AI safety research under Jan Hendrik Kirchner
